address: J2H6+VPJ, Place Ben Youssef, Marrakesh 40000, Morocco
J2H6+VPJ, Place Ben Youssef, Marrakesh 40000, Morocco
review:
Before visiting this museum, I read mixed reviews, with some people complaining that the collection on display wasn’t worth the ticket price (50 MAD as of July 2026). Personally, I find it difficult to put a price tag on culture, so I will simply share my impressions and some pictures of the elements that caught my eye.

Housed in a former noble palace near the Ben Youssef Madrasa, this museum is a perfect example of a venue where the building itself is a major part of the attraction. It features a beautiful inner courtyard with three fountains, and walls adorned with traditional zellij tilework, intricate stucco decorations, and Arabic calligraphic motifs. Among its many rooms, the historic hammam is particularly worth highlighting.

As for the exhibits, the collection displays a mix of contemporary art and traditional artifacts, ranging from jewelry and costumes to weapons and pottery.

J2H6+VPJ, Place Ben Youssef, Marrakesh 40000, Morocco
review: Muzeum Marrakeszu zlokalizowane jest przy Placu Ben Youssef. Prezentuje historyczną broń, biżuterię oraz ceramikę w oszałamiających wnętrzach dawnego pałacu Dar Mnebhi z XIX wieku. W głównym dziedzińcu znajduje się ogromny ważący 1,2 tony mosiężny żyrandol. Podziwiać tam można precyzyjne mozaiki, rzeźbione sufity z drewna cedrowego i marmurowe fontanny. W bocznych salach wystawione są tradycyjne przedmioty z różnych regionów Maroka, w tym berberyjska biżuteria, ceramika z Fezu, broń, kostiumy oraz historyczne monety. Oryginalne pomieszczenia pałacowe zostały przekształcone w sale wystawowe dla sztuki współczesnej. Muzeum czynne jest codziennie od godziny 9:30 do 18:00, a bilet dla osoby dorosłej kosztuje 50 MAD. Naszym zdaniem muzeum warte odwiedzenia.

address: J2H6+VPJ, Place Ben Youssef, Marrakesh 40000, Morocco
review:
Il museo di Marrakech si trova nell’antico palazzo dove un tempo visse Mehdi Mnebhi, il ministro della difesa del sultano Moulay Abdelaziz.

Precedentemente il palazzo era stato utilizzato come collegio femminile.

La parte principale del museo è costituita dal suo cortile, attenzione alla grande lampada, e dalle sale che lo circondano. In queste sale sono esposte le collezioni del museo, di ceramica, armi, tappeti e altri oggetti tradizionali del Marocco.

All’interno dell’edificio si può visitare anche l’hammam tradizionale e una sala con esposizioni temporanee.

address: J2H6+VPJ, Place Ben Youssef, Marrakesh 40000, Morocco
J2H6+VPJ, Place Ben Youssef, Marrakesh 40000, Morocco
review:
We had a wonderful visit to the Marrakech Museum. The architecture of the building alone is worth the detour; it is a stunning example of Moroccan craftsmanship and provides a beautiful, serene setting for the exhibits.
The collection is quite interesting, featuring several notable historical pieces. We were particularly impressed by a traditional Moroccan kaftan dating back several centuries, which is remarkably well-preserved. Another unique feature is how the former hammam has been repurposed into art galleries the layout is clever, and the paintings on display there are magnificent.
During our visit, an artist was on-site creating personalized cards and bookmarks with our names. It was a very thoughtful touch and provided us with a high-quality, bespoke souvenir of our time there.
Lastly, the welcome was excellent from start to finish. The staff is professional and attentive, making the museum very accessible and enjoyable, even for those visiting with children. I highly recommend including this museum in your itinerary.

address: J2H6+VPJ, Place Ben Youssef, Marrakesh 40000, Morocco
review:
A stunning architectural masterpiece! The Marrakech Museum (Musée de Marrakech) is a must-visit, especially for the Dar Menebhi Palace's central courtyard. The history on display—from ancient ceramics to Moroccan coins—is fascinating, but the Moorish architecture, intricate zellige tilework, and the massive hanging brass chandelier are the real stars.

Pro Tip: I visited on a weekday and there was no line, making it much more peaceful than the nearby Ben Youssef Madrasa. You can buy tickets easily at the entrance (approx. 70 MAD). Don't forget to look up—the cedar wood carvings on the ceilings are incredible!"

review:
كانت الجيوش المغربية تتعلَّم السباحة في هذا الحوض الكبير بمراكش قبل التوجه شمالا. لعبور الأندلس ، وتم تشييد صهريج المنارة سنة 1157 م من طرف الحاج يعيش المالقي الذي كان من علماء ومهندسي القرن 6 هـ / 12 م بأمر من السلطان الموحدي، وقام السلطان السعدي زيدان بإضافة الحدائق والأسوار منذ القرن 17م فيما قام السلاطين العلويون بتوسيع المَعلَمة وحدائقها الرائعة التي تحيط بالصهريج ومنارته التاريخية.
أما بالنسبة للصهريج؛ فأبعاده تفوق 150 مترا في العرض و 200 مترا في الطول.
استخدم لتدريب الجنود على السباحة إضافة إلى أدواره في سقي الخيول وري الأراضي الزراعية.
رحم الله اجدادنا العظماء اصحاب الانجازات و الانتصارات و التاريخ المجيد

review:
I visited Menara Gardens a while ago during my time in Marrakech, and it was honestly a peaceful and enjoyable experience overall. The place has a very calm atmosphere that makes it perfect for escaping the noise and crowds of the city for a little while. What I liked most was the simplicity and authenticity of the gardens. The large basin with the beautiful pavilion in the background creates a classic Moroccan view that feels timeless, especially with the Atlas Mountains visible in the distance on clear days.

The area is spacious and great for walking, relaxing, and taking photos. There are many olive trees and open spaces that give the location a natural and refreshing feeling. It is a good place to visit with friends, family, or even alone if you just want some quiet time. I also appreciated how accessible the location is from the city center.

The reason I am giving 4 stars instead of 5 is because some parts could be better maintained, and there are not many activities or things to do besides walking around and enjoying the scenery. Still, that peaceful and traditional atmosphere is exactly what many visitors are looking for.

Overall, Menara Gardens is definitely worth visiting if you are in Marrakech and want to experience one of the city’s historic and relaxing spots.

review:

address: Dar el Bacha, Rte Sidi Abdelaziz, Marrakech 40000, Morocco
review:
An exceptional experience! We arrived at 9:00 a.m. and waited an hour for the doors to open at 10:00 a.m., so we were the first ones inside.
The service was outstanding, and the staff were incredibly attentive and welcoming. I highly recommend their breakfast set menu for 240 MAD. If you visit, don't miss the lychee and raspberry croissants they are, without a doubt, the best croissants I've ever had!

To top it all off, you can finish your visit with a tour of the museum located on the same site.

Overall, it's an experience I'd definitely repeat the next time I'm in Marrakech!

review:

address: Dar el Bacha, Rte Sidi Abdelaziz, Marrakech 40000, Morocco
review:
This place is a dream.
We went around 3 pm and there was no queue, we walked right in. There was an hour wait for the cafe but we didn’t mind as we went to the museum.
An hour was not enough for us as we took lots of pictures and spent time looking through all the exhibits, so we went back after the cafe.
As there was 4 of us, the staff made sure we all sat together. Yasin helped us choose the right coffee and once the coffee came it was a whole experience of how to have it with cream, how much sugar to add. We had croissants and other pastries and everything was delicious. We never felt rushed at any point and the staff made sure we were looked after all the time. Also not just the serving staff but everyone including the guards in the museum, the people in the shop, everyone was friendly and welcoming.
The bill was not a lot either and considering that this was a whole coffee experience as well as a museum visit. I have paid more for very bad coffee in the UK
Definitely a must visit

address: Dar el Bacha, Rte Sidi Abdelaziz, Marrakech 40000, Morocco
review:
A very memorable and happy experience.
Getting in is not easy. This is what worked for us: We showed up around 10AM and got our names on the "4-hour" waiting list - then visited Le Jardin Secret for 2 hours - returned to get our buzzer - toured the Dar El Bacha Museum - and the buzzer went off for a total of about 3 hour painless wait.
The service and atmosphere was exceptional. Take time to browse the coffee book. We ended up getting three pots of coffee (about $5 per pot which contains about 2.5 cups). Didn't sleep well but would do it again!
We heard they will be opening new locations in London and New York. I don't know if the magic of this place can be transported though.

I am really disappointed that such a wonderful and very popular place has such a staff. I work with people, and I was also in the hospitality industry, and that’s why I’m writing this to you

address: Dar el Bacha, Rte Sidi Abdelaziz, Marrakech 40000, Morocco
review:
Visited as part of a Morocco multi-day tour, and Bacha Coffee was one of the highlights of our time in Marrakech. From the moment you enter the magnificent Dar El Bacha Palace, the experience feels special. The setting is exceptional, with traditional Moroccan architecture, intricate zellige tilework, carved cedar wood, elegant courtyards, and a calm atmosphere that feels far removed from the busy medina outside. The coffee selection is unlike anywhere else we have visited, with hundreds of varieties from around the world, all served with great attention to detail.

The service was professional and welcoming throughout our visit. The pastries were beautifully presented, and the coffee itself was excellent. This is not a place for a quick drink. It is a place to slow down, enjoy the surroundings, and appreciate one of Marrakech's most iconic coffee experiences. Arriving early is a good idea, as queues can become long during busy periods.

The team at Visit Morocco Tours often recommends Bacha Coffee to travellers looking for a memorable stop in Marrakech, and after experiencing it ourselves, we understand why.

address: Dar el Bacha, Rte Sidi Abdelaziz, Marrakech 40000, Morocco
review:
The coffee and service once inside was fantastic, and the venue is beautiful - worth a visit.

2 issues: 1) one of the security guards was extremely rude to us for no apparent reason 2) the queuing system is frustrating - you queue (we waited 30 minutes) to get inside. This costs 10 MAD per person. Then, you have to wait inside the garden area and this can take 1-2 hours. So we left and returned, paid another 10 MAD each entry, waited another 30 minutes and then got in. The wait didn’t feel too long but if you’re not prepared to queue, pay, leave, return in 1/2 hours, pay again, then it’s probably not worth the visit

My advice: skip the morning rush and come around 2 PM instead. You’ll likely walk in without fighting the crowds and wasting your time standing in line.

address: Dar el Bacha, Rte Sidi Abdelaziz, Marrakech 40000, Morocco
review:
Very disappointing experience at Bacha Coffee.
Before visiting, we called to ask about reservations and were told that bookings were not accepted. Based on that information, we went directly to the café, only to be told at reception that the next available table would be in 3 hours and that they could put our name on a waiting list.
This raises an obvious question: if customers are expected to wait several hours for a table, why not allow reservations in advance and manage capacity more efficiently? Being told that bookings are not possible, only to arrive and face a 3-hour wait, isfrustrating and feels like poor customer management.
A venue this popular should have a better reservation system and provide clearer information to customers before they make the trip.

address: Dar el Bacha, Rte Sidi Abdelaziz, Marrakech 40000, Morocco
review:
Une expérience absolument inoubliable ! Le café Bacha est un véritable joyau de Marrakech. Dès l'entrée, on est émerveillé par le cadre somptueux et l'élégance des lieux. Le service est irréprochable, digne des plus grands établissements, avec un personnel attentif, professionnel et chaleureux.

Le musée du Palais Dar El Bacha est tout simplement extraordinaire. Il met magnifiquement en valeur l'artisanat, l'histoire et la richesse de la culture marocaine dans un cadre architectural exceptionnel. Chaque salle est une invitation à découvrir le savoir-faire et le patrimoine du Maroc.

Un immense bravo à toute l'équipe pour cette expérience unique. Que ce soit pour savourer un café d'exception ou pour découvrir un lieu chargé d'histoire, je recommande cette adresse sans la moindre hésitation. Un incontournable à Marrakech !

address: Dar el Bacha, Rte Sidi Abdelaziz, Marrakech 40000, Morocco
Dar el Bacha, Rte Sidi Abdelaziz, Marrakech 40000, Morocco
review:
A truly unforgettable coffee house experience in the heart of Marrakesh — one that quite honestly left me speechless.

From the moment I entered, it felt less like a café and more like stepping onto a film set carefully designed for a Hollywood production. Every detail, from the lighting to the ambience, was orchestrated to create an atmosphere of quiet sophistication and theatrical charm.

The coffee selection was nothing short of extraordinary. Beans sourced from every corner of the world were on offer, each cup prepared with evident care and precision. Whether you prefer something bold and earthy or smooth and aromatic, the range caters to both connoisseurs and curious travellers alike.

Equally impressive was the pastry selection. The cakes and pâtisseries were presented with such artistry that they almost seemed too beautiful to eat. Each item felt considered, balanced, and crafted with a level of finesse you would expect from a top-tier patisserie in Paris or Vienna rather than a café in Marrakesh.

What truly elevated the experience, however, was the staff. Dressed in immaculate, starched white uniforms, pressed to razor-sharp perfection, they embodied discipline and pride in service. The deep red fezzes added a striking and elegant cultural touch, creating a visual identity that was both traditional and theatrical.
Their professionalism was exemplary — attentive without being intrusive, polished without feeling artificial. In many ways, the establishment feels like a training ground for hospitality at the highest level, a place where every gesture, from serving coffee to presenting pastries, is performed with intention and grace.

In short, this is far more than a coffee house. It is an experience — a masterclass in presentation, service, and atmosphere that lingers in your memory long after you leave.
200 out of 10 .

review: Souk Semmarine is the pulsing, vibrant heart of the Marrakech medina. This bustling, covered market artery stretching north from Jemaa el-Fnaa is packed with a dazzling array of leather goods, vibrant textiles, and handmade crafts.
जय हिंद 🇮🇳
Сложно что-то выделить из общей массы переулков, поэтому просто наброс фотографий с района. Район прекрасен!

Mucha variedad de artículos artesanales, algunos de uso diario y otros decorativos. Los comerciantes aceptan negociar el precio y son amables. Los pasillos principales están cubiertos por lo que no estarás expuesto al sol. Se recomienda precaución con los objetos personales.

Il Souk Semmarine è uno dei souk più famosi e visitati della Medina di Marrakech, nonché una delle vie commerciali più rappresentative della città. Situato vicino alla celebre piazza Jemaa el-Fnaa, è caratterizzato da un susseguirsi di botteghe tradizionali che vendono tessuti, abiti marocchini, tappeti, articoli in pelle, gioielli, spezie e oggetti di artigianato locale. Passeggiare sotto le coperture del souk permette di vivere l’atmosfera autentica dei mercati storici marocchini, tra colori, profumi e il continuo movimento di commercianti e visitatori. Pur non essendo un sito UNESCO autonomo, fa parte della Medina di Marrakech, riconosciuta come Patrimonio UNESCO, ed è una delle esperienze più iconiche e rappresentative della cultura marocchina.

Beaucoup d'artisanat exposé sur plusieurs centaines de mètres !

I visited Souk Semmarine in Marrakech a while ago, and it was truly one of the most authentic and lively experiences I had in the city. The atmosphere there is full of energy, colors, sounds, and traditional Moroccan charm. Walking through the narrow streets of the souk feels like entering another world filled with history, culture, and local craftsmanship.

What impressed me the most was the incredible variety of products available. From traditional clothing, handmade leather goods, spices, lanterns, carpets, jewelry, and souvenirs to beautiful artisanal decorations, there is something interesting in every corner. The craftsmanship and attention to detail in many of the shops are remarkable and reflect the richness of Moroccan heritage.

The souk is also a great place to experience the real spirit of Marrakech. The interactions with local merchants, the traditional architecture, and the vibrant atmosphere make the visit memorable and unique. Even if you are not planning to shop, simply walking around and discovering the different sections of the market is an experience worth having.

I also appreciated the lively ambiance, especially during the evening when the area becomes even more animated with visitors, street activity, and the smell of traditional Moroccan food nearby. It creates a very special atmosphere that captures the essence of the old medina.

I am giving Souk Semmarine 5 stars because it offers a true cultural experience and an unforgettable glimpse into traditional Moroccan life. It is one of the must-visit places in Marrakech for anyone who wants to discover the city’s authentic spirit, local art, and vibrant atmosphere.

A lot of pros and cons on this for sure. Definitely come once if you're staying in Merrakesh. Only bring cash. Only bring local currency. Keep your bills in separate pockets so they don't see how much money you have. Don't show money until you agree on a price. Bring exact change if you can a lot of people will give you wrong change by "accident".  Most sellers are selling mass produced factory products. Very few are selling hand made items from morroco but they do exist. It can be hard to navigate. Don't overspend. If you feel the price of an item is too high just leave. You'll see how quickly they drop the price when you leave. Overall it's a bit sad, the locals need tourists dollars to survive, and it's clear that some are respectful and some just see you as a full wallet. This is understandable but it can be overwhelming to be stuck in a big market like this being pulled left and right. Would recommend buying spices textiles and rugs. These seem to be the best items I saw. If you're buying an expensive hand made item understand the prices and don't be scared to offer 30-40% of the asking price. Do they flat out say no, this is a sign they have a product that is actual worth something. If you leave and they say sorry I can't go that low without folding, it means it's more honest. If they come down to 30 or 40% without much effort it's definitely not worth it and you should leave that shop! Good luck and try not to get scammed!

Souki to gęsta sieć uliczek ze straganami w starej części Marrakechu - Medinie. Zlokalizowane są niedaleko słynnego placu Jemaa el-Fnaa. To jedno z najbardziej klimatycznych miejsc w Marrakeszu. Można tam kupić typowe marokańskie wyroby, tj. lampy, ceramikę, dywany, przyprawy, wyroby skórzane, pamiątki, biżuterię i wiele innych. Obowiązuje zasada targowania się gdyż ceny wyjściowe są mocno zawyżone. Należy uważać na osoby oferujące różnego typu pomoc, ponieważ gdy im na to pozwolimy, oczekują zapłaty. Pomimo potężnego zatłoczenia, souki to miejsce, obowiązkowe na mapie miasta.

Souk Semmarine is a must-visit in the heart of the medina. It’s perfect for discovering traditional Moroccan crafts, colorful , and unique souvenirs. Take your time to explore the narrow alleys, enjoy the lively atmosphere, and don’t hesitate to bargain it’s part of the experience. Even a short visit is enough to feel the authentic spirit of Marrakech!

Mais en réalité ce sont que des contrefaçons et il vont donc gonflé les prix X2 ou x3 encore plus si vous êtes un touriste la bas les locale sont privilégiés.je ne vous recommande pas d'aller acheter au souk . Aller juste vous balader pour l'expérience elle en vaut le coup mais pas l'argent .

Super expérience au souk de Marrakech !

Franchement, c’est un endroit incroyable que je recommande à 100 %. Dès qu’on y entre, on est plongé dans une ambiance unique : les couleurs, les odeurs, l’animation… tout est vivant et authentique. On a vraiment l’impression de découvrir un autre monde.

Les stands sont hyper variés : épices, vêtements, objets artisanaux, souvenirs… il y en a pour tous les goûts. Même si on ne compte rien acheter, juste se balader dans les ruelles vaut largement le coup. Chaque coin réserve une surprise.

Les commerçants sont accueillants et savent mettre l’ambiance. Oui, ils négocient, mais ça fait partie de l’expérience et ça reste toujours dans la bonne humeur. C’est presque un jeu !

Mention spéciale pour les odeurs d’épices et de nourriture qui donnent envie de tout goûter. On découvre plein de nouvelles choses, c’est super enrichissant.

Bref, une visite incontournable à Marrakech. Que ce soit pour acheter, se promener ou juste profiter de l’ambiance, le souk est un passage obligé !
Very emotionally draining buy also exillerating. Be ready to haggle like your like depends on it, have fun and be respectful

The complete helter-skelter of shopping in the souks of Marrakech is experienced here. Teaming with energy, full of tourists, vendors, chancers, mopeds and hand carts the experience will blow your mind. Bargins and rip offs to be had at every corner. Make sure you haggle hard. Enjoy
Muitas lojas de comércio local.

Locale stupendo con fantastici nebolizzatori per contrastare il caldo, servizio impeccabile e cibo buonissimo e di altissima qualità. Consiglio il tortino con caramello, datteri e zafferano. Menzione d’onore anche per il pesce! Davvero soddisfatti, mix tra tradizione e innovazione. Anche per vegetariani ottimo.

review: Une mauvaise expérience dans ce restaurant pourtant bien noté. Difficile à trouver, le plat chers 240 dirham et pas bon, eau plate du robinet javelisée chère à 30 dirham pour 70cl. C'est "no mad"
response_of_owner:
Merci d’avoir pris le temps de partager votre retour.

Nous sommes sincèrement désolés d’apprendre que votre expérience n’a pas répondu à vos attentes. Notre restaurant est situé au cœur de la médina, ce qui peut effectivement le rendre un peu difficile à trouver lors d’une première visite.

Concernant l’eau, il ne s’agit pas d’eau du robinet. Nous servons une eau filtrée, purifiée et dont le pH est contrôlé, produite sur place dans des bouteilles en verre réutilisables dans le cadre de notre engagement à réduire les déchets plastiques. Le tarif est de 30 MAD pour une bouteille de 1 litre.

Nous regrettons également que votre plat ne vous ait pas convaincu. Notre cuisine propose une interprétation moderne des saveurs marocaines, mais nous comprenons que cela puisse ne pas correspondre aux goûts de chacun.

Nous vous remercions pour votre retour, qui nous aide à continuer à nous améliorer.

review: Enjoyed dinner here — definitely get the topmost rooftop at sunset. I thought this was one of the better rooftop experiences in the city. They also offer you a djellaba in the winter to keep warm, which is a nice touch. The Nomad Burger and Saffron Date Cake were the standout dishes for my partner and me. It's definitely a touristy spot, but people flock to it for a reason. Missed out on trying their coffee (popular Bloom Coffee), but heard that's good too. I'd recommend reserving ahead of time, and asking for a spot at the topmost rooftop.
response_of_owner:
Thank you so much for your wonderful review 

We’re delighted to hear that you enjoyed your dinner and the rooftop experience at sunset. It’s great to know you appreciated the capes we provide during the cooler months to keep our guests comfortable.

We’re especially happy that the Nomad Burger and our Saffron Date Cake were highlights of your meal. 

We hope you'll have the chance to try our Bloom Coffee on your next visit. We look forward to welcoming you back soon

review:
Vam sopar al NOMAD durant la nostra estada a Marràqueix i va ser una experiència molt recomanable. El menjar és excel·lent, amb plats molt ben elaborats i una presentació acurada. El servei va ser atent i professional en tot moment.

La terrassa és un dels grans atractius del restaurant, amb unes magnífiques vistes sobre la medina, especialment al capvespre. L'únic aspecte que milloraria és la il·luminació, que ens va semblar una mica escassa, i el preu, una mica més elevat que en altres restaurants de la ciutat.

Cal tenir present que no serveixen begudes alcohòliques, un aspecte habitual en molts establiments del Marroc.

Si busqueu bona cuina, un ambient tranquil i una terrassa espectacular, és una aposta segura.
response_of_owner: Moltes gràcies per la vostra magnífica ressenya! Ens alegra saber que vau gaudir de la nostra cuina, del servei i, especialment, de la terrassa amb vistes sobre la medina. Agraïm també els vostres comentaris sobre la il·luminació i els preus, ja que sempre ens ajuden a continuar millorant. Esperem tenir el plaer de donar-vos la benvinguda de nou ben aviat a Nomad!

review: I do not like writing bad reviews but can only give this place 2 stars (and that being generous). The roof top terrace was inviting and our visit started well with ordering some drinks and starters. The three starters were delicious. Sadly, that’s when our positive experience ended. The waiters were not attentive and it was difficult to get their attention to order some more drinks. We then had a long wait for our slow-cooked lamb confit main course dishes , that we were all excited to try. (And at 240 MAD per dish, we were expecting something delicious) When they arrived, they were not hot, so we had to send them back to be re-heated. When they eventually returned, we tried them, only to discover they were extremely salty. Despite trying to be polite, we couldn’t eat it so had to call the waiter over and politely explain that they needed to taste it. With no conversation, he took them back to the kitchen. With no explanation, he bought back the menus and told us to order another dish. By this point we had lost our appetite but persevered. We therefore tried to play safe and ordered the Moroccan-spiced chicken. When it arrived, we were not impressed to find this was also salty. We tried to mix the salty chicken with the cous cous to dilute the salty taste and by this point we were fed up and ready to leave. We left at least a third of our chicken and told the waiter that we also found this salty - no response. We asked for the bill. They did not charge us for the extra salty lamb dishes but gave no apologies for the salty main course or the poor/slow service and cold food. They even had the cheek to include their compulsory service charge. We could have argued over this but just wanted to get out of there, so paid and left. I would say this place is a tourist trap. They clearly get lots of booking so aren’t bothered about providing good food or decent service. I would avoid this restaurant. There a plenty of of other roof top restaurants that know how to look after you.
response_of_owner:
Thank you for taking the time to share such detailed feedback. We're pleased to hear you enjoyed the rooftop setting and the starters.

We're genuinely sorry to learn that the rest of your experience fell short of your expectations. It's disappointing to hear that both the food and the service did not leave you with the impression we strive to create. While we understand that tastes can vary, your comments regarding the seasoning, waiting times, and communication have been noted and will be shared with our team for review.
We're glad our team removed the lamb dishes from your bill after your feedback. Regarding the service charge, it is clearly indicated on our menu and contributes to supporting our service team and maintaining the level of hospitality we aim to provide. That said, if a guest is genuinely dissatisfied with the service, we always encourage them to raise it with a manager during their visit so we can address the situation appropriately.

We appreciate your feedback and wish you all the best in your future travels.

review: Ristorante stupendo, con una terrazza magnifica affacciata sulla piazza che, nonostante la posizione centrale, regala un’atmosfera tranquilla e rilassante, perfetta sia per una serata tra amici sia per una cena romantica. Molto apprezzata l’attenzione verso chi soffre di celiachia o intolleranza al glutine, grazie a un’ampia scelta di piatti dedicati. Una menzione speciale va all’agnello, cotto per molte ore e davvero eccezionale: tenerissimo e ricco di sapore. Personale attento e professionale, ambiente curato e bagni impeccabilmente puliti. Consigliatissimo anche per ammirare uno splendido tramonto su Marrakech
response_of_owner: Grazie mille per la splendida recensione! Siamo felici che abbia apprezzato la nostra terrazza, l'atmosfera, la selezione di piatti senza glutine e, soprattutto, il nostro agnello cotto lentamente. Le sue parole sul servizio e sull'attenzione ai dettagli significano molto per tutto il nostro team. Speriamo di accoglierla nuovamente molto presto!

review: In Marokko musste ich zum ersten Mal in einem Restaurant eine Service Charge bezahlen. 9% !!.  Für mich ist das eine Frechheit. Das Essen war zwar lecker, aber Service gehört für mich zum Restaurantbesuch dazu und sollte nicht extra berechnet werden – vor allem nicht bei ohnehin schon hohen Preisen. Deshalb werde ich Restaurants mit einer solchen Zusatzgebühr nicht weiterempfehlen!!!
response_of_owner:
Vielen Dank für Ihr Feedback. Es freut uns, dass Ihnen das Essen geschmeckt hat.

Wir bedauern, dass Sie mit der Servicegebühr nicht einverstanden waren. Eine Service Charge ist jedoch keineswegs etwas, das es nur in Marokko gibt – sie ist in vielen Ländern der Welt üblich, auch wenn sie in Ihrem Heimatland möglicherweise nicht gängige Praxis ist.

Unsere 9 % Servicegebühr werden transparent auf unserer Speisekarte ausgewiesen und ermöglichen es uns, unserem Team faire Arbeitsbedingungen und einen hohen Servicestandard zu bieten. Sollten Sie mit dem Service unzufrieden gewesen sein, hätten Sie uns dies gerne vor Ort mitteilen können – in solchen Fällen prüfen wir selbstverständlich gerne die Entfernung der Servicegebühr.

Vielen Dank nochmals für Ihren Besuch und alles Gute.

review: Heeeerlijk gegeten. Marokaans eten met een moderne twist, maar absoluut authentieke smaak. Tip: reserveer van tevoren een plekje buiten
response_of_owner: Bedankt voor uw geweldige review! Wat fijn om te horen dat u heeft genoten van onze moderne interpretatie van de Marokkaanse keuken en de authentieke smaken. Bedankt ook voor de handige tip over het reserveren. We hopen u snel weer te mogen verwelkomen!

review:
Nómad es el ejemplo perfecto de cómo una vista espectacular puede compensar una experiencia gastronómica mediocre.

El rooftop y el atardecer sobre la Medina son magníficos, pero la comida está lejos de justificar los precios. Lo más sorprendente es que cuesta aproximadamente el doble que Café des Épices (del mismo grupo) para una calidad muy similar.

El servicio es correcto, sin más, y tampoco está a la altura de lo que uno espera pagando esos precios.

La sensación final es que no pagas por la cocina, sino por la vista. Sales contento por el sunset y el ambiente, no por lo que comiste. Una pena, porque con una mejor propuesta gastronómica podría ser un lugar excepcional y no solo uno de los rooftops más fotografiados de Marrakech.
response_of_owner:
Gracias por compartir su opinión. Nos alegra saber que disfrutó de la terraza y de las vistas sobre la Medina.

Lamentamos que la experiencia gastronómica no haya estado a la altura de sus expectativas. Nuestro concepto y nuestra carta son diferentes a los de Café des Épices, con una propuesta centrada en ingredientes de alta calidad, una cocina más elaborada y un servicio de mesa completo, lo que explica la diferencia de precio. Entendemos, sin embargo, que cada comensal tiene sus propias expectativas en cuanto a sabor y relación calidad-precio.

Agradecemos sus comentarios y esperamos tener la oportunidad de darle la bienvenida de nuevo en el futuro.

review: Beau rooftop. La cuisine est savoureuse.

The type of restaurant which left us hungry in spite of ordering a starter, main, and a dessert. The portion of the food is for a 7 yr old child. I ordered fish of the day and 25% of the plate was occupied by a half a lemon! Our total bill was 680 MAD! Eating here is the complete opposite of what you would expect from a culture that loves feeding people. We were left hungry and we had to go to Grand Bazaar restaurant an hour later.

Overpriced. Mediocre service. Mediocre Food.
response_of_owner:
Thank you for taking the time to share your feedback. We're sorry to hear that your experience did not meet your expectations.

Our dishes are intentionally designed as refined, contemporary plates that focus on quality ingredients and careful preparation rather than large portions, and we understand that this style may not suit everyone's preferences. We appreciate your comments regarding value and portion sizes, as guest feedback is always taken into consideration.

We hope to have the opportunity to welcome you again in the future.

review: Average, go elsewhere. Staff was pleasant enough and the ice tea was delicious. Food was bland, tiny portions and at a bill nearing 700MAD. Everywhere else I have eaten in Morocco I left satisfied and full. If your leaving a restaurant still hungry whats the point? Tourist trap, avoid.
response_of_owner:
Thank you for sharing your feedback. We're pleased to hear you enjoyed our homemade iced tea and found our team welcoming and attentive.

We're sorry that the food didn't meet your expectations. Our menu is designed around fresh, seasonal ingredients and balanced flavors, and while our portions are intended for sharing or enjoying as part of a multi-course meal, we understand that preferences can vary. We also appreciate your comments on value and will share them with our team.

We hope to have the opportunity to welcome you again and provide an experience that better reflects what so many of our guests enjoy.

review: The food is not great. The beef with prunes was 1/3 cow fat and low on the seasoning. The chicken with morocon spices was a little on the dry. Too expensive for the quality and quantity. Great view to zinc sheet roofs and ruined buildings. The staff was very nice and available.
response_of_owner:
Thank you for taking the time to share your feedback. We're pleased to hear that you found our team welcoming and attentive.

We're sorry that the dishes you chose didn't meet your expectations. Our recipes are prepared with fresh ingredients and are designed to offer a balanced interpretation of Moroccan flavors, though we understand that tastes and expectations can vary. We also appreciate your comments regarding the portion and value, as guest feedback is always taken into consideration.

We hope we'll have the opportunity to welcome you again for a better experience.

review:
Based on recommendations from travel influencers, TV features, and our Airbnb host, we were excited to dine here and booked an 8:00 PM rooftop reservation hoping to catch the sunset. We were seated promptly, which we appreciated. Our table was along a row of two-tops and unfortunately on the opposite side of a wall that partially blocked the sunset view. While neither of these things significantly impacted our experience, they did make the rooftop setting feel a little less special than we had anticipated.

The main disappointment was the food. Given the restaurant’s reputation, we expected a more memorable dining experience. We completely understand and respect that alcohol is not served, though we were surprised there wasn’t a dedicated mocktail menu. I ordered the apple green tea infusion, which tasted good enough but was quite mild and reminded me mostly of iced mint tea (for 400 MAD).

The dishes we tried were nicely presented, but overall we found them lacking in flavor. Even the spiced Moroccan chicken was more subdued than expected. The bread, which is an additional charge, was served cold and seemed less fresh than we would have liked.

Overall, the experience wasn’t bad, but it didn’t live up to the high expectations created by the many recommendations we received. For us, the restaurant felt somewhat overhyped compared with the quality of the food and drinks.
response_of_owner:
Thank you for taking the time to share such detailed feedback. We're pleased to hear you appreciated our rooftop setting and that your table was ready upon arrival, although we're sorry the seating arrangement didn't offer the sunset view you had hoped for. As sunset tables are in high demand, we aren't always able to guarantee a specific location on the terrace.

We're also sorry to learn that the food and drinks didn't meet your expectations. Our menu focuses on fresh, seasonal ingredients and balanced flavors, which may differ from more traditional interpretations of Moroccan cuisine. We also offer a selection of homemade juices, infusions, and non-alcoholic drinks rather than a dedicated mocktail menu.

We appreciate your comments regarding the bread and portion sizes, and we'll be sure to share your feedback with our team. We hope to have the opportunity to welcome you again and provide an experience that better reflects what so many of our guests enjoy.

Gracias por su visita y por tomarse el tiempo de compartir su experiencia.

review:
I'm really not sure why this was highly recommended.

Nice staff, mediocre food, high prices, and I felt unwell afterwards.

Bread was not tasting like it was just made to order, maybe earlier in the shift - but pan flatbread should be fresh.

Most of our arugula blew away in the wind when it was served at the table (and not replaced), but also did not seem fresh for half of the pieces. Arugula is possibly a bad choice in this weather as the flavor gets bad more quickly than other greens.

Roasted pumpkin was cold, which could make sense, except it wasn't clear when ordering that it was cold & ultimately something tasted unwell about it. Ok, the roasted pumpkin was roughly 1 part puree, 1 part from one pumpkin, and 1 part from another pumpkin. Technically only one smelled/tasted bad but really surprising at a high end place. We did not eat anymore than a little bit and smelled the pieces to confirm.

Eggplant was the best of the dishes we got.
Lentils with goat cheese was second best.

Maybe this was just a bad and busy day. So, if you had a good time or want to go, that's fine. But, I would not recommend it as a favorite.

We even were like "ok, it's going to be a little touristy and some reviews are negative, but so many people can't be wrong [for those who say to go]. For us, they were wrong.

View was great. And the experience of being in the shops and then jumping up to the terrace was nice.

Edit: feel free to read the owners response. They would do well to spend less time replying to reviews and more time changing how they operate. The pumpkin was spoiled, not just cold. The reason I brought up cold pumpkin is because it takes a higher level of operational professionalism to take something, roast it, then chill it and serve many hours later.  Other parts the owner highlighted was how I said the staff was nice. 😑. It would take a lot to throw staff under the bus. Don't focus on trying to change my words, actually try to just acknowledge what doesn't work and change how you run the place 🤷.
response_of_owner:
Thank you for taking the time to share such detailed feedback.

We’re truly sorry to hear that your experience did not meet your expectations. While we’re happy that you appreciated the staff, the views, and the atmosphere of the terrace, we regret that several dishes left you disappointed.

Your comments regarding freshness, temperature of the dishes, and overall quality have been taken very seriously and shared directly with both our kitchen and management teams for review. What you describe is not the experience we aim to provide.

Regarding the rooftop conditions, as an open-air terrace in the heart of the medina, wind can sometimes affect the experience, though we understand your frustration about the arugula not being replaced.

Thank you again for your honest feedback. Even if our concept and modern twist on Moroccan cuisine may not suit every palate, comments like yours help us continue to improve.

review:
Restaurant permettant d’avoir une belle vue sur la médina, ainsi que la mosquée Koutoubia. Le service est top, avec un personnel souriant et réactif.
Niveau nourriture, l’entrée était bonne et les plats étaient corrects. Peut-être nous nous attendions à davantage de saveurs pour ces derniers à la vue des tarifs, notamment du fait de l’application de frais de service de 9%.
response_of_owner:
Merci beaucoup pour votre retour !

Nous sommes ravis que vous ayez apprécié la vue sur la médina et la Koutoubia ainsi que la qualité du service de notre équipe.

Nous prenons également bonne note de votre remarque concernant les saveurs des plats et vos attentes par rapport aux tarifs. Notre cuisine propose une interprétation moderne de la cuisine marocaine, mais nous comprenons que chacun puisse avoir des attentes différentes en matière de goûts et de générosité des saveurs.

Concernant les frais de service, ceux-ci sont indiqués sur notre menu et sont répartis entre les équipes de salle et de cuisine.

Merci encore pour votre visite et votre retour constructif

review: Mooi dakterras restaurant in de medina. Vlotte en op zich vriendelijke behandeling. Maar prijs/kwaliteit van het eten was echt niet goed. Kleine porties, niet echt bijzonder qua smaak. Dus voor een drankje leuk om heen te gaan maar qua eten zijn er veel betere opties. Ik ben inmiddels voor de 4e keer in Marrakech dus heb al veel restaurants bezocht.
response_of_owner:
Bedankt voor uw uitgebreide feedback.

Fijn om te horen dat u het dakterras en de sfeer van het restaurant heeft gewaardeerd, evenals de vriendelijke service van ons team.

Het spijt ons echter dat de prijs-kwaliteitverhouding en de gerechten niet aan uw verwachtingen voldeden. Ons concept richt zich op een moderne interpretatie van de Marokkaanse keuken met zorgvuldig geselecteerde ingrediënten, maar we begrijpen dat smaken en verwachtingen kunnen verschillen, zeker wanneer u Marrakech al goed kent.

We waarderen uw eerlijke mening en danken u voor uw bezoek.

review:
I had the Calamari Sofrito — a perfectly executed dish that highlights both technique and bold Moroccan flavors. The calamari were tender with a light char; the harissa brought warm, smoky heat while the anchovy added a satisfying umami backbone without tasting fishy. Fresh ginger and cilantro brightened each bite, and the braised eggplant and zucchini provided soft, savory balance and a pleasant contrast in texture.

Service was attentive and friendly, prices felt fair for the quality and portion sizes.

Would recommend for an enjoyable, contemporary take on Moroccan flavors — especially if you like seafood with a spicy, aromatic twist.
response_of_owner:
Thank you so much for this wonderful and detailed review !

We’re truly delighted to hear how much you enjoyed the Calamari Sofrito and appreciated the balance of flavors, textures, and ingredients in the dish. Your description perfectly reflects the spirit of our cuisine, a contemporary interpretation of Moroccan flavors with carefully selected products and bold seasoning.

We’re also very happy that the service, portions, and overall experience met your expectations.

Thank you again for your kind recommendation, and we hope to welcome you back very soon for another memorable meal

review: Food is excellent but by Moroccan standards it’s very overpriced and portions are small. You’re paying for that rooftop view. Do not order the bread - 10 MAD for two small pieces of the worst bread we had all trip.
response_of_owner:
Thank you for your feedback. We’re very pleased to hear you enjoyed the food and the rooftop atmosphere.

Regarding pricing, we understand that perceptions of value can vary from one traveller to another. Morocco is not necessarily a “cheap” country, and like any touristic destination, you will naturally find a wide range of restaurants, from very low-cost local spots to mid-range and higher-end dining experiences. Our restaurant positions itself in the latter category, with pricing that reflects not only the rooftop location, but also the quality of ingredients we source, the preparation of our dishes, the level of service we provide, and the overall experience we aim to offer.

As for portions, our menu is designed around balanced plates rather than oversized servings, though we appreciate that expectations can differ from guest to guest.

We are however sorry to hear the bread did not meet your expectations. Your comments have been shared with the team and taken into consideration.

Thank you again for visiting us and for taking the time to leave your review.

review:
احد المطاعم الشهيرة التي تمتاز بإطلاله جميلة على الاسواق وأسطح المنازل في مراكش.
طلبنا كان فطائر الكوسا، نوماد برغر، فيليه سمك مقلي بصوص البيستو.
الاطباق بشكل عام جيدة ولكن الافضل كان البرغر.

الجلسات بالسطح كانت جميلة خاصة بعد غروب الشمس ومع اشعال الفوانيس.
والقائمين على الخدمة متعاونين فقد تعاونوا معنا على تغيير الطاولة الى اخرى ذات اطلالة.
يتطلب حجز مسبق عبر الموقع قبل بيوم على الاغلب.
response_of_owner:

ونشكر لكم أيضاً كلماتكم الجميلة عن فريق الخدمة، يسعدنا دائماً مساعدتكم لتجربة مريحة ومميزة.

review: This really well maintained. Absolutely perfect.  Make sure to get some change to donate to the needy beggers outside after the prayers.   There is no tickets, it's free for Muslim man and women to enter and pray.  Non Muslims can view the mosque from outside free.

マラケシュのランドマーク的存在で、街のどこからでも見える印象的なモスクです。

イスラム教徒以外は内部に入ることができませんが、正直なところ、外から眺めるだけでも十分楽しめました。夜にライトアップされた姿も綺麗です。

個人的には、マラケシュは何日もかけて観光するほど見どころが多い街ではないと感じました。クトゥビーヤ・モスクを見て、ジャマ・エル・フナ広場を散策し、お土産を買うくらいがちょうど良いかもしれません。

「マラケシュは絶対に外せない！」というよりは、「モロッコに来たので一度見ておく」という感覚の観光地でした。
Внутри не побывал к сожалению, зато снаружи всё очень напоминает архитектуру в городе Севилья.
Great place for Salah.
Great place
Imposible no verla, porque domina la plaza. Es muy bonita.
La mezquita de Marrakech en la noche se ve muy
Super a faire
Très belle mosquée Allahuma y barik fih ❤️

The Koutoubia Mosque is a magnificent building kept in exceptionally beautiful condition. Located right in the heart of the city center, it is a very lovely and inviting landmark. The surrounding parks and gardens are beautifully organized and well-kept, making it a truly pleasant place to spend time during the early morning or later in the evening.

​However, during most of the day, there are quite a few homeless individuals and intimidating people hanging around the area, which unfortunately causes the place to lose some of its magical charm. Nevertheless, this doesn't take away from the immense historical and architectural value of the building itself. It is definitely a must-see, but timing your visit makes a big difference!

De Koutoubia‑moskee is de grootste en bekendste moskee van Marrakech en staat vlak bij het plein Jemaa el Fna, in het hart van de oude stad. Ze werd in de 12e eeuw onder de Almohaden gebouwd en herbouwd; de naam verwijst naar de vele boekverkopers die vroeger rond de moskee hun manuscripten en boeken verkochten.
Het complex beslaat ruim 5000 m², met een hypostyle gebedshal met 17 schepen en een eenvoudige, brede patio, maar het meest opvallend is de vierkante minaret van ongeveer 70–77 meter hoog, die als oriëntatiepunt boven de hele stad uittorent. Deze toren, met zijn zandkleurige steen, uitgesneden bogen en banden van groene en witte tegels, vormt samen met de Giralda in Sevilla en de Hassantoren in Rabat een “familie” van klassieke Almohadminaretten.

Tra i simboli più riconoscibili di Marrakech, la Moschea della Koutoubia – جامع الكتبية è uno di quei monumenti che lasciano il segno ancora prima di raggiungerli.

Con il suo elegante minareto alto quasi 70 metri, domina lo skyline della città ed è visibile da molti punti della medina. Mi ha colpito la sua imponenza, ma soprattutto l'armonia delle proporzioni e la semplicità dell'architettura, che la rendono uno degli esempi più belli dello stile almohade.

Anche se l'interno è accessibile solo ai fedeli musulmani, passeggiare nei giardini che la circondano permette di ammirarla da diverse prospettive e di godersi un momento di tranquillità a pochi passi dal vivace centro di Marrakech.

Una curiosità: costruita nel XII secolo, la Koutoubia ha ispirato monumenti famosi come la Giralda di Siviglia e la Torre Hassan di Rabat, diventando uno dei modelli dell'architettura islamica occidentale.

Vale la pena visitarla sia di giorno che al tramonto, quando la luce calda esalta il colore della pietra.

Consiglio pratico: raggiungila poco prima del tramonto e fermati nei giardini circostanti: è uno dei momenti più suggestivi per ammirare il monumento e scattare splendide fotografie.

La Moschea della Koutoubia è una tappa imperdibile per chi visita Marrakech e vuole conoscere uno dei monumenti più iconici del Marocco. 🕌🇲🇦✨
Sehr schön
Beautiful historic Mosque that has been around for over 1000 years. I went there for Jumma Prayer and it was a memory I won’t forget. Highly recommend it.

Kutubiye Camii, Marakeş’in simgesi haline gelmiş en önemli tarihi yapılardan biri. 12. yüzyılda inşa edilen cami, Endülüs ve Mağrip mimarisinin en güzel örneklerinden biri olan zarif minaresiyle dikkat çekiyor. Çevresindeki geniş bahçeler ve tarihi atmosfer, burayı daha da etkileyici kılıyor. Şehrin kültürel mirasını yakından hissetmek ve Marakeş’in tarihine tanıklık etmek isteyen herkesin mutlaka görmesi gereken bir yer. Hatta daha da ileri götüreyim. Bu ülkede gezilmesi gereken yerlerde ilk beşe girer.

A Mesquita Koutoubia é um dos monumentos mais emblemáticos de Marrakech e vale a pena passar por lá para apreciar a sua arquitetura e envolvência.

A visita é apenas pelo exterior, uma vez que a entrada não é permitida a não muçulmanos, mas ainda assim é um local muito bonito para ver, fotografar e sentir um pouco da história da cidade.

Recomendo incluir no roteiro, sobretudo por estar numa zona central e próxima da Praça Jemaa el-Fna.

**“The soul of Marrakech rising above the Medina ✨🕌
Koutoubia Mosque stands as a timeless symbol of history, faith, and beauty. Its majestic minaret watches over the city like a guardian, guiding travelers through the maze of the old Medina.
Built in the 12th century, it is more than a mosque — it is the heartbeat of Marrakech, where the call to prayer blends with the rhythm of daily life. At sunset, when the light touches its walls, the city feels like pure magic.
A place where history, peace, and Marrakech’s spirit come together in one unforgettable view 🧡”**

Una visita muy interesante para entender la historia de Marrakech. Hay que tener en cuenta que gran parte del palacio se encuentra en ruinas y hoy solo se conserva su estructura, pero aun así es fácil imaginar la enorme majestuosidad que debió tener en su época.

Además, el recinto cuenta con un pequeño museo donde se exponen piezas recuperadas, recreaciones en vídeo del palacio original y explicaciones sobre su construcción y la historia de Marrakech. Los paneles están en árabe y francés, pero con Google Translate es muy sencillo seguir las explicaciones y disfrutar mucho más de la visita.

​If you are expecting a fully intact, richly decorated site like the Bahia Palace, you might be disappointed. El Badi is essentially a massive, open-air ruin rather than an active palace. While the sheer scale of the historical remains is impressive and has its own rustic beauty, the overall experience feels underwhelming for the price of admission.

​If the entry fee were halved, it would be a very fair and highly recommended historical stop. As it stands, the price-to-value ratio is hard to justify when compared to other stunning sights in Marrakech. It is a beautiful ruin to walk through, but definitely adjust your expectations before buying a ticket!

A marrakech sono tutti gentili, tranne in questo posto. Il signore alla biglietteria è di una maleducazione inaudita, oltre a non parlare un briciolo d'inglese che è una follia essendo un sito turistico. Abbiamo acquistato i biglietti online, ma per un malfunzionamento del sito ci hanno mandato la ricevuta ma non il QR code. Siamo andati al Bahia Palace e facendo vedere la ricevuta ci hanno fatto entrare senza problemi. Qui invece il signore ha risposto continuamente "ticket" "ticket", alzando la voce quando provavamo a spiegargli la questione. Tutto il personale fuori diceva che non si poteva fare niente, però intanto i soldi ce li hanno scalati.
Da evitare assolutamente, anche perché da quanto poi ci hanno spiegato gente che usciva è in ristrutturazione e non si vede nulla.

Una stella mi sembra già anche tanto. Abbiamo pagato il biglietto on-line, ci hanno preso i soldi ma non ci è mai arrivato il QR code perché ci mettono 24 ore a mandartelo. Nonostante la ricevuta di pagamento, non ci hanno fatto entrare. Il signore anziano all’ingresso che si occupa di guardare i biglietti ha iniziato ad alzare la voce contro di noi e si è rifiutato di parlare in inglese. Scandaloso, considerando che è un posto turistico.

Het El‑Badipaleis, zijn de indrukwekkende resten van een Saadisch feest en ontvangstpaleis uit het eind van de 16e eeuw in de kasba van Marrakech, vlak bij Jemaa elFnaa en de Saadische graven. Sultan Ahmad alMansur liet het bouwen na zijn overwinning op de Portugezen bij de “Slag van de drie koningen” betaald met losgeld en oorlogsbuit, en gebruikte de rijkste materialen: Italiaans marmer, goud, halfedelstenen. Het paleis is een van de meest bezochte historische plekken van Marrakech en vormt vaak het decor voor festivals, concerten en folkloristische voorstellingen, vooral in de zomer.

I had an incredible experience visiting El Badi Palace. This historic site is one of the most fascinating attractions in Marrakech and offers a unique glimpse into Morocco's rich history and architecture.
The palace ruins are impressive, with vast courtyards, ancient walls, and beautiful gardens that create a truly memorable atmosphere. Walking through the site, I could imagine the grandeur and beauty that once made this palace one of the most magnificent in Morocco.
The views from the terraces are stunning, offering panoramic perspectives of Marrakech and the surrounding areas. The site is well maintained, and there are plenty of opportunities to take amazing photos.
I highly recommend visiting El Badi Palace for anyone interested in history, culture, and architecture. It is a must-see landmark in Marrakech and an unforgettable part of any trip to Morocco. 🇲🇦✨🏰

La Mamounia is one of the most extraordinary hotels and experiences we’ve ever had. From the moment you step through the doors, you can feel its rich history and timeless elegance. It truly is a destination in itself and a must-stay at least once in a lifetime.

The breakfast was spectacular. We enjoyed the traditional Moroccan lamb breakfast, but the buffet was simply out of this world, offering every imaginable delicacy, beautifully presented.

A special thank you to Ali, who has been with the hotel for over 30 years. His warmth, hospitality, and genuine care made our stay even more memorable.

The gardens and pool are absolutely breathtaking, creating a peaceful oasis in the heart of Marrakech, and we loved our beautiful room.

We were celebrating our 20th wedding anniversary, and upon arriving in our room we were surprised to find it decorated with rose petals, along with a bottle of champagne and a delicious cake. We had never requested this, making the gesture even more thoughtful and touching.

The spa is an experience not to be missed. The facilities are stunning—more like walking through a magnificent palace than a spa—and the traditional hammam treatment was exceptional.

Finally, the location is ideal, just a short walk from the Medina while still feeling like a secluded retreat.

La Mamounia is a true gem, combining beauty, history, impeccable service, and unforgettable hospitality. It exceeded every expectation, and we cannot wait to return.

I am deeply disgusted and insulted by the utterly unprofessional, discriminatory, predatory, and corrupt behavior of the staff at La Mamounia.On my first visit, I was wearing casual attire with running shoes, along with large pearl earrings and a vintage Chanel necklace (with the logo removed to be discreet). The hotel strictly enforced its dress code and refused my entry. However, at that exact moment, I witnessed European guests in short pants and sandals entering without any issues. Experiencing this blatant double standard, I confronted the staff on the spot, asking: "Are you refusing me because I am Asian (Yellow)? Is this racial discrimination?" They offered no valid justification.The following day, I returned for a rematch. This time, I was dressed flawlessly in an unbranded Armani silk blouse, paired with the same Chanel necklace and pearl earrings—a look that fully respected the hotel’s prestige.However, instead of professional courtesy, a male staff member at the garden café (Le Pavillon de la Piscine) crossed a dangerous line. He began inappropriately hitting on me, aggressively asking for my room number, and persistently offering to "show me around the city of Marrakesh."To make matters worse, when I left, the gate security staff colluded with a local taxi driver to scam me. They charged me 70 Dirhams for a distance that normally costs only 20 Dirhams. Later, when I dined at another reputable local restaurant and shared this experience, the professionals there told me directly: "For La Mamounia's security staff to do such a thing means you were targeted and looked down upon simply because you are an Asian woman."La Mamounia's staff hid behind biased regulations one day to discriminate, behaved like predatory street men the next, and finally operated like cheap scammers to steal money from a solo Asian female guest. Did they think they could exploit and humiliate me just because I am Japanese?This completely ruined my visit. This hotel does not represent luxury; it represents deep-seated racial bias, sexual harassment, and financial corruption. Management must immediately investigate the staff at the gate and the garden café. This systemic discrimination and criminal collusion must be exposed.

Almeno una volta nella vita bisogna soggiornare al La Mamounia. Il lusso dell’architettura e il meraviglioso parco, nonché la gentilezza di tutti, fanno di questo Hotel uno dei posti imperdibili per un soggiorno indimenticabile nella splendida città rossa.

review:
I liked the hotel: a magnificent garden, excellent restaurants, and legendary guests. I would not call it perfect — for example, the handle on my balcony door came off, although it was fixed the same day. Still, the overall level is very high.

The only thing that truly irritated me was being asked at every turn whether I was a hotel guest or a visitor. That is understandable, but not when it happens three times in 15 minutes. The reason, apparently, is that tourists are brought here by bus for guided tours, and the hotel tries to prevent them from wandering around freely. Nevertheless, actual guests should not be treated this way.

review: La Mamounia is undeniably beautiful. The gardens are stunning, the property is iconic, and every staff member we encountered was warm, gracious, and professional.||Unfortunately, our stay did not live up to the level of luxury we expected given the price point.||Arrival & Room Experience||Upon arrival, we were assigned a room with a shower design so small that water spilled onto the bathroom floor.  When asked about it they showed us how to roll the towels to mitigate the water issue. After raising the issue with management, we were moved to a suite. While we appreciated the accommodation, it came with an additional charge and, according to management, was the only option available if we wanted a larger shower that would not flood the bathroom.||The suite was more spacious, but we still encountered several frustrations. The room technology and lighting controls were confusing and unintuitive, making simple tasks unnecessarily difficult. Small but important details were also overlooked, such as the lack of milk or cream for in-room coffee.||We were also surprised to find outdated hair dryers. At a hotel of this caliber, we expected modern, high-performance models comparable to those found at other leading luxury properties. While seemingly minor, details like these contribute significantly to the overall guest experience.||Service Recovery||What disappointed us most was the limited service recovery after we raised our concerns. Everyone we interacted with was courteous and professional, but we left feeling that our feedback to management was acknowledged rather than truly resolved.||As two women traveling together, there were also moments when we felt our concerns were not addressed with the level of attention we expected. While we cannot know the reason, it contributed to an overall feeling of being less supported than we anticipated.||Overall Impression||La Mamounia remains a breathtaking destination worth visiting for its beauty, history, and atmosphere. However, for the rates charged, we expected a more polished, guest-focused experience, with greater attention to comfort, functionality, and responsiveness when issues arise.||Beautiful hotel, but not the seamless luxury experience we had hoped for.

Wow. Okay. I really just wanted to see Marrakesh (what a great name for a city, btw) and after doing some research, realized that THIS is the hotel to stay here. (I think there’s one other fancier royal palace converted to a hotel and all the 5-star luxury chains you can think of, but this is IT!)|The fact that Winston Churchill loved here 90 years ago and Paul McCartney stayed here in the 70s and wrote a song about tells you something. It’s got charisma. I loved every minute of my stay. And I will say, if I had stayed anywhere else in Marrakesh I know I would have been disappointed, even if it were at a Four Seasons.|Marrakesh has been cleaned up considerably in the last few years and now it resembles a terracotta or salmon pink Moroccan Rancho Mirage (California), all six lane, perfectly paved boulevards stretching as far as the eye can see. Now, I like a cleaned up city (I wasn’t too happy to go home to LA especially) but it may have taken some of the rough and tumble charm I was expecting at the Medina. Tangier 15 years ago felt much more exotic.|That said, La Mamounia was the treasure I didn’t know about before this trip. I had heard about it over the years but didn’t know it could be this amazing. And it was.|Flying from Lisbon was quick and the airport was very nice (at least coming… going back was rougher). We were picked up the hotel’s Range Rover and got very nice check-in treatment (perhaps as a result?). It wasn't that expensive but the airport is really close. We didn’t have any bags (just backpacks) as this was a quick, short trip for us from Portugal but that really confounded the bell staff. I don’t think they had ever seen anyone check in with basically no baggage. It become a running joke.|We had moved our travel dates up a few days to impending horrible weather (105 F heat) that was coming so ended up in the most basic room as that was all that was available online. Fortunately, we were upgraded to a nice corner suite which we thought was great… windows on three sides, a little reading alcove with more windows and a giant private patio. Though it was almost right on the main street, you didn’t get any street noise. (Though we were right behind the employee break area so you got a lot of chatting and cigarette smoke at certain times, which kind of ruined my bath one day but it was atmospheric and I survived). I was happy at the upgrade but at check-out I got the impression they couldn’t believe we could have liked it as much as we did. We did! It was beautiful. But what else have you got?|The hotel was built in the late 1920s in an art deco nouveau style and set on 60 lavishly landscaped acres of historic 300 year old olive trees in what was formerly a royal garden. How could you not enjoy it? It is also exceptionally well-lit at night. The hotel has been recently renovated and they kept what was beautiful and improved on anything that was dated. There are still bathroom tiles from the 20s (or at least they look like they could be). I think the lighting could still use some work... never figured out how to fully use it.|This is hands down the most photogenic hotel I’ve ever been to, and I’ve luckily been to some nice ones. |When we arrived, we were seated in the huge, main reception area on large, cushy couches under a great lighting fixture which has to be seen to be believed while waiting to be checked in. Then we were taken down a few darkened halls with amazing wood detail and torchieres to our room. It was a hike but was worth it. |As soon as we admired the suite and settled in, we went off to check out the hotel. |It is gorgeous… each area was more stylish than the next. After enjoying the lavish gardens from a park bench, we managed to snag the best table at the main outdoor bar and settled in the for the evening. Our server Abdul was fun and friendly and made the evening enjoyable… the next night we did the same thing and were served by Douiri, another delightful server, who got an assist from Abdul. The food was great… some of the best fish and chips I’ve ever had and we were in a desert. Both evenings were delightful, in large part to the friendly staff. |The people watching was pretty great, too. Lots of French people in fancy resort wear with too much money enjoying themselves and smoking/vaping more than you’d think. The next morning, breakfast was served by the pool. It was gorgeous. The food was… sad to say… just okay and in some instances cold and lacking in the buffet… but the service was mostly very good (a few outliers… including one guy who kept giving us the check before we had any food on our plates). Still, what a location for breakfast. The pool was getting a little crowded by the time we left each day. I only ventured into it only once and I was a little intimidated by the pool “boys”… mostly unsmiling men… who set up your lounge chair for you. Fortunately, I had the good luck of being served by Toufik… what a super nice guy! The famous pool was really enjoyable except for the really boring but loud conversation held by the few American businessmen at the hotel in the shallow end (surprise!) who were surprisingly annoying. I left early because I couldn’t listen anymore. I missed the French smokers/vapers!|One day we had lunch at the Italian restaurant. The main waiter was a little unhappy with the fact that we only split a pizza. We were a little unhappy the pizza was so bad. And it was. And expensive, too. We ended up splitting a club sandwich in the bar the next day before our flight which had obviously been pre-made and was ice cold. That said, it was tasty and didn’t make us sick, as we had feared it would. The club sandwich at the pool looked better. So, other than some servers at breakfast and the Italian restaurant waiter, we had great service and except for our fish and chips, the food was just okay. Still, it didn’t matter. This place is magical.|The gym is large and in the middle of the gardens, so a hike away on a hot day. Alas, though it was beautiful and well-equipped, its high ceiling made it apparently hard to air condition. It was one of the hotter (in the temperature sense) hotel gyms I’ve been to. We made a quick visit to the hotel gift shop looking for a baseball cap… alas none fit but the tall gentlemen who runs it could not have been more charming.|We only left the hotel once to walk to the Medina. We had to see it and we saw it and were back in 90 minutes. Um. Worth seeing, I guess? We didn’t get much of a greeting coming or going. I think the bellmen have seen too many tourists who aren’t guests. They’re very good at keeping them away from most guest-only areas but non-guests can come in for the restaurants and linger on the grounds. It wasn’t really a problem during our stay but sometimes you do get asked if you're a guest, which is never my favorite feeling. |Some employees who deserve special mention… Nordin, our driver; Khalid, who checked us in, Hadijah, our butler; Abdul, our great server the first night; Douri, our great waiter the second; super friendly Toufik at the pool, and Imane at the front desk. Plus the gift shop gentleman. In fact, almost everyone else we came into contact with was pleasant, too… I just didn’t get their names. |BTW… there are house cats everywhere. It’s the hotel’s thing and it’s really kind of nice. And I’m not necessarily a cat person.|The gardens and the hotel are just so beautiful words can’t do La Mamounia justice but others have tried… even in song. So I've thrown in quite a few pics. When the sweet woman at the front desk, Imane, asked if we’d come back, I said absolutely! I meant it. And she said it would be in a better room. Why not?  I think that tells you there are a lot of regulars who come here… it seemed to be very French during our time… and I totally understand why. |This hotel is not like any other. It is truly one-of-a-kind. I look forward to coming back anytime (except summer) and I would highly recommend staying here if you’re ever in Marrakesh… I can’t imagine staying anywhere else in this city. It’s that good.

review:
La Mamounia is truly one of the most beautiful hotels I've ever visited. From the moment you walk through the doors, you're surrounded by stunning Moroccan architecture, elegant décor, and immaculate gardens that create a peaceful oasis in the heart of Marrakech. The service was exceptional throughout our stay – attentive, professional, and always delivered with a warm smile.

The food was outstanding, with a fantastic selection of restaurants and a breakfast experience that was a highlight in itself. The facilities, especially the spa and pool areas, were world-class and provided the perfect place to relax and unwind.

Its location is ideal, just a short walk from the Medina, yet it feels completely secluded and tranquil. While it is certainly a luxury hotel with luxury prices, the overall experience, atmosphere, and attention to detail make it worth every penny. A truly unforgettable stay, and I would highly recommend La Mamounia to anyone visiting Marrakech.

review:
Nous venons à Marrakech depuis de nombreuses années et avons toujours considéré La Mamounia comme un lieu emblématique, synonyme d’élégance et d’excellence. C’est donc avec beaucoup de tristesse que nous repartons aujourd’hui avec une grande déception.

Nous étions venus déjeuner au restaurant italien de l’hôtel en famille. À notre arrivée, l’accès a été refusé à notre mère de 70 ans au motif qu’elle portait des sandales ouvertes élégantes, pourtant parfaitement adaptées aux 45 degrés qu’il faisait à Marrakech ce jour-là et portées avec une tenue très soignée. Le plus choquant n’a pas été la règle en elle-même, mais la manière dont cela a été formulé, puisqu’il lui a été suggéré d’aller acheter des chaussures dans la boutique de l’hôtel avant de pouvoir entrer. Nous avons trouvé cela profondément humiliant et irrespectueux.

Après discussion, nous avons finalement été autorisés à entrer, et il faut reconnaître que le service du restaurant s’est ensuite montré professionnel et agréable. Malheureusement, le malaise créé dès l’entrée a marqué toute l’expérience.

Concernant le déjeuner lui-même, nous avons également été déçus par la qualité des plats, qui ne correspondaient pas du tout au niveau d’excellence attendu d’un établissement comme La Mamounia. La pâte à pizza était quasiment impossible à couper, et la sauce de la salade vraiment étrange et déséquilibrée. Rien n’était catastrophique, mais rien n’était mémorable non plus, ce qui est particulièrement dommage compte tenu du standing et des prix pratiqués.

Le plus regrettable est que nous avons pris le temps d’écrire à la réception ainsi qu’au concierge il y a maintenant 10 jours afin de partager notre déception de manière constructive. Nous n’avons jamais reçu le moindre retour, pas même un simple message d’excuse ou de considération. Pour un établissement de ce niveau, ce silence est tout simplement incompréhensible et très décevant.

Ce qui nous attriste le plus est le contraste entre l’image chaleureuse et raffinée que nous avons toujours associée à La Mamounia et l’expérience vécue aujourd’hui.

Nous avons d’ailleurs dîné ensuite au restaurant Sesamo du Royal Mansour, où nous avons vécu une expérience absolument remarquable, aussi bien au niveau de l’accueil que de la cuisine et du sens du détail.

Une expérience malheureusement très décevante à La Mamounia. Nous ne reviendrons plus et privilégierons désormais le Royal Mansour lors de nos prochains séjours à Marrakech.
response_of_owner:
Nous vous remercions d’avoir pris le temps de partager votre expérience, et nous sommes sincèrement désolés de lire votre déception. Ce que vous décrivez ne reflète en aucun cas les standards d’accueil et de service que nous nous efforçons d’offrir chaque jour à nos hôtes.
Afin de pouvoir comprendre plus en détail ce qu’il s’est passé et d'apporter les réponses appropriées, nous vous invitons à nous écrire à l’adresse suivante : informations@mamounia.com. Votre retour nous est précieux et nous souhaitons vivement échanger avec vous.
En espérant avoir l’opportunité de regagner votre confiance,
Cordialement,

I visited Dar El Bacha Museum in Marrakech a while ago, and it was honestly one of the most beautiful and memorable places I explored in the city. From the moment I entered, I was impressed by the architecture, the elegance of the design, and the peaceful atmosphere that surrounds the entire place. The museum perfectly reflects the richness of Moroccan culture, art, and history in a very refined and authentic way.
The details inside are absolutely stunning. The traditional Moroccan craftsmanship, colorful zellige tiles, carved wood, fountains, and Andalusian-inspired decoration make every corner worth admiring. It truly feels like stepping into another era. I spent a lot of time simply appreciating the beauty of the building itself, even beyond the exhibitions.
The museum is very well organized, clean, and calm, which makes the visit enjoyable and relaxing. I also appreciated how the displays were presented in a way that allows visitors to learn more about Moroccan heritage and historical objects without feeling overwhelmed. The atmosphere is peaceful and respectful, making it a perfect place for people who enjoy culture, history, and architecture.
Another highlight was the famous café inside, which adds even more charm to the experience. Sitting there after visiting the museum felt very special and elegant.
I am giving Dar El Bacha Museum 5 stars because the experience exceeded my expectations in every way. It is not only a museum, but also a masterpiece of Moroccan architecture and heritage. I highly recommend visiting it if you are in Marrakech and want to discover one of the city’s most beautiful cultural treasures.

address: Rue Yves St Laurent, Marrakech 40000, Morocco
review:
I felt the museum was overpriced and didn’t really constitute a full museum experience. The exhibitions were quite short and lacked enough substance about both the art and Yves Saint Laurent himself. I actually feel I learned much more from doing my own research on Wikipedia. I’m not saying there was nothing to see, just far less than I expected for the admission price.

My favorite part was the small cinema where a documentary about Yves Saint Laurent is shown. It gave much more insight into both his work and his personality than the exhibition itself.

I also felt that the museum seemed designed to funnel visitors toward the gift shop, which appears halfway through the visit.

Finally, I found the staff rather uptight, and the strict no-photography policy felt unnecessary and somewhat elitist. It leaves you feeling watched, as staff members are constantly checking whether anyone is taking pictures. I can photograph the Mona Lisa, but not this? It almost makes you wonder whether the policy exists to distract from how limited the exhibition actually is.

review: Je me présente je suis monsieur anto membre de la chambre du syndicat de la haute couture française je suis voisin avec le musée j'ai envoyé très souvent des gens et toujours la même histoire du personnel à l'entrée qui est très désagréable qui est mal élevé donc je pense qu'avec les pauvres jeunes qui ont besoin de travailler il faudrait penser à changer parce que je pense que quand même pour l'image de marque d'un grand couturier comme Yves Saint Laurent ça ne le fait pas du tout j'ai fait aujourd'hui l'expérience le 2 juillet 2026 pour voir si les gens et les invités que j'avais envoyé au musée m'avait dit qu'ils avaient été toujours très mal reçu et je pense que si Monsieur Bergé qui était un grand ami serait encore vivant je pense que ça serait pas passé comme ça alors s'il vous plaît pour l'image du de ces deux personnages essayer de faire quelque chose j'ai une maison juste à côté de chez vous laisser dommage que nous nous pouvons pas faire et profiter par exemple du café pour se rafraîchir et je reviendrai la prochaine fois en espérant que ça vous aurez fait le nécessaire pour qu'il n'y ait plus d'incidence ce style là parce que même si nous avons amusé nous sommes au Maroc et que des personnes comme moi qui habillons la famille royale depuis de nombreuses années je pense que ça pourrait déclencher un incident je ne suis jamais mal reçu aucune part sur le royaume je suis quelqu'un de très poli et courtois donc je vous remercie à très bientôt

address: Rue Yves St Laurent, Marrakech 40000, Morocco
review:
Por poco que te guste la moda este es un Museo que no te puedes perder. Yves Saint Laurent historia viviente de la moda del s.XX. Intemporal la colección Pierre Bergé sobre YSL. Cómo puede alguien haber coleccionado tantas prendas de un genio como YSL. El Museo tiene una parte interactiva también interesante. Justo al lado está el Jardin Majorelle, también una buena visita. (Se pueden comprar las entradas al mismo tiempo).
Ah, te aconsejo que compres entradas para primera hora de la mañana y te des un paseo desde la Kashba hasta el Museo, conocerás un Marrakech distinto al que hayas visto hasta entonces (te adjunto algunas fotos).Ah, como siempre en los Museos, o guía o audioguía, que te permite entender mejor lo que estás viendo!
