address: Rue Yves St Laurent, Marrakech 40000, Morocco
review:
Yves Saint Laurent Müzesi Marakeş’in tarihçesi şöyle:
Yves Saint Laurent, Fas’a ilk kez 1966 yılında Pierre Bergé ile geldi. Marakeş’in renkleri, ışığı, kumaşları ve geleneksel havası onu çok etkiledi. Bu yüzden Marakeş, onun tasarımlarında önemli bir ilham kaynağı oldu.
Müze, bu bağı anlatmak için 2017 yılında Marakeş’te açıldı. Gerçek konumu Fas / Marakeş, yani İtalya’da değil. Müze, Jardin Majorelle’ye çok yakın bir yerde bulunur.
Binanın dış cephesi modern Fas mimarisi tarzındadır: kızıl-tuğla renkli, sade ama lüks bir yapı. İçinde Yves Saint Laurent’in kıyafetleri, çizimleri, fotoğrafları, aksesuarları ve moda arşivi sergilenir.
Kısaca: Bu müze Marakeş’te, Yves Saint Laurent’in Fas’tan aldığı ilhamı ve moda mirasını anlatan özel bir müzedir.

review:
There are restaurants you visit for the food, and then there are restaurants you remember for the entire experience. Comptoir Darna is firmly the latter. From the moment you walk through the doors, you’re immersed in an atmosphere that perfectly captures the magic of Marrakech; elegant interiors, warm lighting, live music, and an energy that builds throughout the evening.

The hospitality was exceptional. Every member of the team was welcoming, attentive, and genuinely passionate about making sure every guest had a memorable night. Service was polished without feeling formal, and despite how busy the restaurant was, everything ran seamlessly. Reservations are definitely recommended, and it’s easy to understand why. This place is one of Marrakech’s most iconic dining experiences.

The food was beautifully presented and packed with flavour, celebrating traditional Moroccan cuisine while also offering international favourites. Every dish felt thoughtfully prepared, making dinner just as much of a highlight as the entertainment itself. I had the chicken curry and it was delicious.

As the evening progressed, the atmosphere completely transformed. Live musicians filled the room before the famous belly dancing performances began, creating an immersive show that had the entire restaurant captivated. It strikes the perfect balance between authentic Moroccan culture and upscale dining, making it feel more like a dinner theatre experience than a traditional restaurant.

As a solo female traveler, I also felt completely at ease throughout the evening. The staff were incredibly respectful and attentive, making me feel comfortable from the moment I arrived. Dining alone never once felt awkward… in fact, the welcoming atmosphere made it easy to relax and simply enjoy the experience.

Considering the quality of the food, the level of service, and the unforgettable entertainment, Comptoir Darna offers excellent value for such a renowned venue. It’s one of those places where you don’t just leave remembering what you ate… you remember the music, the performances, the hospitality, and the feeling of spending an evening immersed in the heart of Marrakech.

If you’re looking for one restaurant that truly captures the city’s vibrant spirit, Comptoir Darna should be at the very top of your list.

review: Le soir quand il fait moins chaud decouvrir toute la magie de Marrakech

Très animé, à faire du vous êtes de passage sur Marrakech. Les vendeurs ne sont pas insistants si vous refusez poliment, en revanche les snacks au centre de la place risquent de vous arrêter tous les 10m.
Por la tarde mucha gente pero se están bien , algo pesados pero es a lo q están, y se regatea todo
Beaucoup trops de monde, il y a beaucoup mieux a visiter au Maroc plus beau et authentique mais pour manger ou boire un thé pendant une soirée c'est sympa

Çok kalabalık kargaşa bir meydan. Hele maymun oynaticilari yılan oynaticilarindan iyice uzak durun. Biraz bak hemen yapışıp para istiyorlar hele birde fotoğraf falan çek hiç şansın yok. Biraz ilerideki pazar sokaklarını gezmek daha güzel.
Es una plaza enorme, muchos puestos de fruta, de tatuajes de genna, y bueno la final del España Argentina en pantalla grande

Super 👍👍 topissime 👍👍
Une ambiance inoubliable
A faire absolument
Comme c'est connu endroit magique
ساحة جميلة غنية بالثقافات المغربية من موسيقى كناوة ولباس تقليدي عريق ورقصات على انغام الموسيقى ومأكولات شعبية وعصائر متنوعة💝👏
Expérience à vivre, bonne ambiance mais BEAUCOUP TROP de bruit..
Divino todo

La piazza di Marrakesh il primo giorno ti mette ansia, il secondo ti conquista e dal terzo giorno non ne puoi più farne a meno. Colori, odori ed emozioni in un concentrato di persone caotico ma coinvolgente. I venditori sono frenetici ma non insistenti. Unica pecca le scimmiette in catene alla mercé dei proprietari che le utilizzano per foto con turisti compiacenti... Veramente triste. Piacevole da visitare anche laattina presto quando si presenta quasi vuota. Nel complesso imperdibile!
Busy and vibrant 😎
Très sympathique on y trouve de tout .
Enorme, caótica, bulliciosa, variopinta... y al mismo tiempo maravillosa e inolvidable.
Endroit bien organisé mais tres sale

Ambiente de feira igual se por vários países, muitas cores, variedade e preços. Vale lembrar para ter cuidado com fotos e vídeos principalmente de pessoas e animais,pois os nativos podem querer recompensas em dinheiro
Inévitable à Marrakech. Il faut y aller le soir pour l'animation.
ساحة للتراث الإنساني العالمي.

La place Jemaa el-Fna est vraiment dépaysante, c'est une expérience unique. L'ambiance est très animée, presque déroutante lors d'une première visite : beaucoup de bruit, de monde et de sollicitations de la part des vendeurs et des artistes de rue.
Il ne faut pas hésiter à marchander, cela fait partie de la culture locale et c'est même attendu. Les jus de fruits sont bons et rafraîchissants, même si certains semblent être allongés avec un peu d'eau.
Le meilleur conseil est de prendre son temps, de rester vigilant avec ses affaires, de profiter de l'atmosphère et de ne pas se laisser impressionner. Que l'on aime ou non, c'est un lieu incontournable de Marrakech qui mérite d'être découvert au moins une fois.

Day and Night are different vibes. During the day you can take pictures with snakes and monkeys, make sure you agree on a price beforehand. Don't fall for "pay whatever you want", they'll end up asking for 100dh when you can pay 20 to 30dh max.

At night there are many music circles, take a picture or video and you'll immediately be approached for change. Usually 10dh is more than enough.

Many juice bars around. It's also the entrance  the market and shops.
Lieu à visiter
Un mundo maravilloso
A night the Jemaa el-Fnaa comes alive with music, candles, food stands etc. In the day quite as you can see from my photo.
انعدام موقف السيارات و ضجيج مبالغ فيه
Miejsce magiczne, każdy powinien to zobaczyć na własne oczy
Waouh j'étais bluffée par cette place et surtout la vente des smoothies et la viande grillée. Il ya toute sortes de boutiques et surtout une forte animation

ساحة جامع الفنا من أجمل الأماكن التي زرتها في مراكش، فهي تنبض بالحياة طوال اليوم، وتزداد جمالًا وحيوية مع حلول المساء. تضم الساحة عروضًا متنوعة لفنانين الشارع، إلى جانب عدد كبير من محلات العصائر الطازجة، والمقاهي، والمطاعم التي تناسب مختلف الأذواق والميزانيات.

ومن أجمل ما يميزها اتصالها المباشر بأسواق المدينة القديمة، حيث يمكن التجول بين الأزقة التاريخية والاستمتاع بالتسوق وشراء المنتجات التقليدية المغربية. الأجواء مليئة بالحيوية، وتعكس الثقافة المغربية الأصيلة، مما يجعلها وجهة لا ينبغي تفويتها عند زيارة مراكش.

أنصح بزيارتها في الفترة المسائية للاستمتاع بالأجواء، مع تخصيص وقت كافٍ للتجول في الأسواق المحيطة بها.

A bustling square filled with treats for all 5 senses. No matter what time of the day or night you visit you will not be disappointed. From food stalls, to music groups, games to tattoo artists, juice stalls to snake charmers, not to mention shops, stalls and cafes, there is just so much going on.  Plenty of people around but overall felt very safe, especially on my 2nd or 3rd visit.
Tourisme trop agressif surtout les restaurant au centre de la place

Place incontournable de Marrakech... on y trouve de tout et l'animation du soir y est spéciale et agréable..
A voir absolument

Busy all day and late into the night. Choose where you eat carefully as hygiene standards not the same as UK.
Monkeys, snakes, beggars, lunatics on motorbikes, you got the lot.
People friendly but if you show the slightest interest be prepared to be hassled.
Whatever they all, offer 25% and beater from there.
I bought a genuine fake Rolex.
Started at 900DAM, got it full 200DAM.
If I had tried harder probably got it for 150DAM but I was eating pizza so couldn't be bothered. Needed a watch as mine gave up the ghost after 2 days of getting here.
About 15 quid? If it only lasts as long as the holiday it will have done what I wanted.
Generally you can have a good time there, just think before you commit.

Una sola plaza, dos ambientes completamente diferentes de día y de noche. Para disfrutar de ambas y de los contrastes que nos presentan. El atardecer desde las terrazas junto a la plaza es espectacular.
Bien animé mais attention ⚠️ surtout pas faire de photos avec les serpent 🐍 ou autres car à tout les coups vous allez vous faire arnaque, évitez les rabatteur

La plaza reúne mercado de menta, especias, cueros, y otras. Ventade jugos de frutas naturales. En las noches vibra con la música de distintos grupos locales que tocan, cantan y bailan para un público que se reúne aldededor en asientos improvisados en un círculo. Concurrida por turistas y habitantes del lugar. Hay mujeres que hacen tatuajes de henna. También hay lugares para comer, tomar un helado. No hay venta de bebidas alcohólicas en este sector.
Molto caotica ma caratteristica
Yılanları be maymunları göremedik ama olsun.
Lieu mythique de Marrakech. À visiter en journée et le soir surtout !

Un endroit idéal pour flâner la nuit,
Manger, faire des achats

Una piazza larga.
Nessuna attrazione possibile, se si escludono di giorno gli incantatori di cobra e le scimmie.
Folkloristica e colorata perché si affaccia sul suk.

Der Platz der Gaukler, hier kann man viel sehen und erleben. Entweder Mann ist mitten drin oder beobachtet das ganze von einer der Cafés am Rand. Meine Empfehlung auf einer der Dachterrasse von dort sieht man den ganzen Platz. Für uns war das ein Riesen Erlebnis und eine ganz neue Welt.
Place pleine d'animation.
Gewoon druk plein, niets bijzonder vind ik. Wel redelijk schoon. Was er overdag misschien daarom niet bijzonder?
Plaza con mercado y zoco lleno de gente, bullicioso pero seguro.

Es muy festiva y puedes ver música, cuenta cuentos y comida local deliciosa, sin embargo, una señora que hacen tatuajes con jena, se acercó a mi, tomó mi mano y sin qué le pidiera empezó a dibujar un tatuaje, después quizá cobrarme y yo no traía efectivo, llamó a otra mujer qye hablaba español y "me obligó a pagarle" de alguna manera, terminé por darle mi bote de pastilla de menta.
جامع الفنا ملتقى الثقافات المغربية

Toujours autant d ambiance , les vendeurs ça va ne sont pas trop embêtant il suffit de ne pas les regarder 😅
Nous avons assisté au match Maroc France de le coupe du monde , c était super encadré, police pompiers , samu , sécurité et tout c est bien passé. Les gens sont très respectueux.
Pintoresca entretenida y original por la noche cobra vida de luces, aromas y colores. Disfruta de un buen zumo natural a un precio muy económico.

A fuir ne caspiller pas votre argent.
En ce moment a Marrakech des travaux et tout est démonter.
Un attrapé touristes.
Aucune offre.
Vous perdez votre temps.

reviewer_name: In Marocco con Laura di Laura Martino
review: La mia seconda casa
Volop in restauratie. Prachtige mozaïek en plafonds
जय हिंद 🇮🇳
review_details: [{'title': 'Visited on', 'value': 'Weekend'}, {'title': 'Wait time', 'value': 'Up to 10 min'}, {'title': 'Reservation recommended', 'value': 'Not sure'}]

review: Bardzo ładne miejsce, jednak w obecnym stanie (remonty po trzęsieniu ziemi) zwiedzanie jest trochę okrojone - nam zajęło jakieś 40 minut z przewodnikiem (którego swoim zdaniem polecam, opowiedział wiele interesujących faktów o historii o architekturze pałacu, których nie widziałem na tabliczkach)
Très beau palais dans l'ensemble, mais dommage que les pièces soient dénuées de mobilier. De plus une partie est en travaux donc non visitable. Visite un peu décevante tout de même.
En renovation et ne le disent pas au client..pas grand chose a voir ne vaut pas le prix

Ein sehr schöner Palast mit einem wunderschönen Garten. Leider sind momentan auf dem Hauptplatz und damit auch die Hauptattraktion Bauarbeiten.
Und da ich davon ausgehe, dass der Eintrittspreis aufgrund der Bauarbeiten nicht nach unten gesenkt wurde, gibt es einen Punkt Abzug. Wenn allerdings alles renouviert ist, ist es mit Sicherheit eine sehr schöne Location und dann sind auch die 10€ Eintritt in Ordnung.
Purtroppo c erano i lavori e non l abbiamo potuto vedere in tutta la sua bellezza.
Visita muy bonita, no cogimos guía

Very nice place to visit. Guides and audio tours are available as you enter. Give yourself at least 45 minutes to explore all of the rooms, gardens and clamazing courtyard at the rear of the palace ( currently being renovated). Amazing details to ceiling and full of history
Well worth a visit while in Marrakech.

正直なところ、私のように歴史や建築にあまり興味がない人にとっては、満足度はそこまで高くありませんでした。

入場料は100ディルハムですが、「そこまで見どころがあるかな？」というのが率直な感想です。美しい中庭や装飾はあるものの、見学時間はそれほど長くなく、価格に見合っているかは人によって意見が分かれると思います。

また、訪問時には施設内の一部で工事が行われており、景観が損なわれている場所もありました。工事中であれば、入場料を少し下げるなどの配慮があっても良いのではないかと感じました。

歴史やモロッコ建築が好きな方には楽しめるかもしれませんが、限られた予算で観光する方は、優先順位を考えて訪れることをおすすめします。
Joli mais pas d'effet waouh par rapport au prix.  On trouve des bâtiments aussi richement décorés gratuitement à d'autres endroits dans Marrakech
Lieu reposant et agréable, malheureusement en travaux au moment de notre passage
El sitio está en obras y es difícil disfrutar su belleza si está todo cubierto o en obra de reparación

Bahia Palace wasn’t anything special, in my opinion. We got our tickets very quickly, but once inside there were lots of tour groups with guides. I would rate this palace 2.5 out of 5. It is beautiful, but many parts are under renovation. Some areas are being restored, while others still need repairs. It was also very crowded, so if you wanted to take photos, you had to wait for people to move out of the way.

I also visited another place that I thought was 100 times better: 📍Ben Youssef Madrasa (Medina, Marrakech). The ticket to this place is cost 50dh.
I would definitely recommend visiting that instead.
10 euros for a palace work in progress. Not worth
Highly recommended!

Très beau palais, ( actuellement en travaux )qui mérite vraiment une visite. La découverte se fait facilement et nous plonge dans une autre époque grâce à son architecture et à son atmosphère

Le seul petit point que j'améliorerais serait d'ajouter des représentations ou des reconstitutions des personnes qui vivaient à cette époque. Cela permettrait de s'immerger encore davantage dans l'histoire du lieu et de rendre l'expérience encore plus vivante

Considerate che l'ingresso a Palazzo El Bahia per 2 adulti e 2 adolescenti (a luglio 2026) viene 400 dirham (circa 40 euro) quando invece per la Madrasa di Ben Youssef abbiamo pagato la metà esatta (200 dirham). Ti aspetteresti di vedere qualcosa delle blasonate 160 stanze ed ettari di giardini e orti. Niente di tutto questo. L'area calpestabile di giardini era limitata a pochi metri quadrati. L'iconico cortile interno 50x30 metri era transennato, aperto solo in parte e soggetto a lavori di ristrutturazione che ne deturpavano l'aspetto. Il percorso turistico è, tutto sommato, breve. Chi ha scritto nelle recensioni che ha fatto delle belle passeggiate in 8 ettari (80.000 mq) deve essere una persona di riguardo dell'ente o un giardiniere perché a noi comuni turisti non ci è stato permesso. La visita non vale la cifra pagata in rapporto ad altri luoghi storici della Medina. Come se non bastasse, la ragazza preposta al controllo biglietti all'ingresso era quanto di più lontano dall'essere una persona ospitale. Non mi porterò questa visita nel cuore.
Very disappointed , we pay full price tickets than inside is closed , only small part open not even 2 minutes visit . Its under construction at the moment .
Um oásis de tranquilidade
Il y a la foule impossible de prendre des photos au de voir tranquillement surtout avec un groupe de touristes
Красиво, только главный двор на реставрации
Tra il quartiere ebraico e il suo una piccola perla Marrakesh
It is a dilapidated palace.
Beautifully restored palace in the heart of the Medina. Well worth a visit even if it's hot - nice & cool inside. Allow about an hour & recommend Beldi Fusion for something to eat /drink afterwards.
Palazzo bellissimo in pieno stile marocchino, peccato solo per la cura dei dettagli che potrebbe essere migliore

Quite expensive and really not much to look at apart from a lot of empty rooms. According to a guide we met who lives within the palace there are 65 rooms, we saw 5.
If you have been to La Hambra in Spain it is about the same.
Don't bother with an audio guide, you won't need it.
Beau site avec malheureusement plusieurs travaux, dommage que des expositions permanentes de mobilier ne soit pas présentes.
De lo mejor de Marrakech

Entree a 100 MAD....un peu cher
Un Surplus Pour l audio guide.
Sinon a faire , tres Jolie Palace. Visite d environ 1 h00.
Esta en obras la parte principal del palacio y cobran lo mismo que si estuviera en condiciones. Ni un solo descuento.
Nice to see, but not very special.

Très beau palais à visiter. Nous sommes allées vers 15h40 un vendredi, attention car ça peut fermer pendant les heures de prière. Nous n’avons pas réserver à l’avance et il n’y avait pas du tout d’attente pour prendre un billet qui coûte 100 MAD pour un adulte. Nous avons payé en espèces. Le palais est très beau avec un sens de visite clair. Il y avait une partie en travaux. Nous l’avons visité en environ 40 minutes mais assez rapidement.
Interesting

Avec get your guide une visite détaillée avec le guide sidi.
Un passionné d'histoire, de Marrakech et du maroc en général.
On a voyagé dans le temps avec des explications sur l'architecture, les fonctions des pièces, les matériaux... une visite à faire absolument ! Bravo sidi

usually a great place to see in marrakesh, they did not bother to mention that a good part of the place was under construction and not accessible. online ticket purchase was not possible and card payment is not possible at entry but it is for the audio guide which does not make any sense. we did not get our moneys worth, would not recommend going at the moment
Un palacio venido abajo, lindo pero no para pagar la entrada que te cobran, no te llevarás nada interesante que sea único u original.
Magnifique palais. Des détailles d'une grande finesse
uno scorcio di cultura marocchina spettacolare
Très bien jolie

Bonito palacio, aunque no me sorprendió tanto como los lugares que visitamos en Fes. Es bonito y de gran detalle arquitectónico, pero a mi parecer la arquitectura que puedes ver en Fes es aún más bonita y detallista.
A partir de junio está en remodelación, por lo que hay varios sectores dentro del palacio que no se pueden apreciar en su plenitud ya que están tapados.
Good site to see

​Magnifique témoignage de l'architecture marocaine et andalouse. Les détails des plafonds en cèdre sculpté, les stucs et les zelliges sont impressionnants, en particulier dans la grande cour d'honneur. La déambulation à travers les différents riads et jardins est très agréable, offrant de superbes jeux de lumière pour les photos.

​Quelques conseils pour la visite :

​Horaire : Privilégiez une visite dès l'ouverture le matin ou en fin d'après-midi pour éviter les grands groupes de touristes et la chaleur.

​Guide : Les pièces étant vides de mobilier et les panneaux explicatifs assez succincts, prendre un guide à l'entrée ou se documenter un peu en amont permet de vraiment apprécier l'histoire fascinante de ce palais du XIXe siècle.

​Durée : Comptez environ 1h à 1h30 de visite.

Rue Yves St Laurent, Marrakech 40090, Morocco
review:
Our visit to Jardin Majorelle was a truly beautiful, uplifting experience and a wonderful, serene contrast to the vibrant hustle of Marrakech. It felt like stepping into an entirely different world, and it was absolutely worth every moment.

​The iconic buildings are strikingly beautiful, and the gardens are incredibly well-kept. What we loved most was how the garden truly came alive if you paused to immerse yourself in its magic; we spotted so many lovely local birds and small wildlife darting through the lush, pristine greenery.

​While it was a slight pity that one or two sections were closed off, which is completely understandable given how some tourists behave, it did not dampen our experience at all.

​As a helpful note for fellow international travelers, we were delighted to find that they graciously accepted our European (Hungarian) student IDs, which granted us a significantly more favorable ticket price. It remains an enchanting, highly recommended oasis that everyone should visit!

address: Rue Yves St Laurent, Marrakech 40090, Morocco
review: Jardin Majorelle, Marakeş’te mutlaka görülmesi gereken en özel yerlerden biri. Bahçenin her köşesi büyük bir özenle tasarlanmış; egzotik bitkiler, dev kaktüsler, palmiyeler ve rengârenk çiçekler arasında yürümek gerçekten huzur veriyor. Özellikle bahçenin simgesi hâline gelen canlı mavi renkli yapılar, yeşilliklerle muhteşem bir uyum oluşturuyor ve fotoğraf çekmek için harika bir ortam sunuyor. Kalabalık olmasına rağmen içeride sakin ve dinlendirici bir atmosfer hissediliyor. Gerçi belirli bir sırada alıyorlar içeri belki onun da etkisi vardır. Doğa, sanat ve estetik tasarımın bir araya geldiği bu bahçe, Marakeş gezimizin en keyif aldığımız duraklarından biri oldu. Eğer yolunuz Marakeş’e düşerse, burayı gezi listenize mutlaka eklemenizi tavsiye ederim. Ben çok beceremedim ama güzel fotoğraflar çekilebilecek bir yer.

review:
Jardin Majorelle
est l’un des endroits les plus beaux et visités de Marrakech 🌿
🌴 C’est quoi ?
Un jardin botanique créé par le peintre français Jacques Majorelle dans les années 1920, puis restauré plus tard par Yves Saint Laurent.
🌺 Qu’est-ce qu’on y trouve ?
Des plantes exotiques du monde entier 🌵🌴
Le célèbre bleu “Majorelle” très photogénique 🎨
Des bassins, fontaines et allées calmes
Le musée berbère (culture amazighe)
⭐ Pourquoi visiter ?
Endroit calme et reposant loin du bruit de la médina
Parfait pour photos et détente 📸
Un des sites les plus iconiques du Maroc

Mientras muchos grupos entraban, se hacían la foto de rigor y seguían su ruta, yo pasé casi dos horas disfrutando de este lugar. Es un sitio para contemplar y disfrutar sin prisas. Si dedicas tiempo a observar los detalles, entiendes por qué es uno de los lugares más especiales de Marrakech.

The Ben Youssef Madrasa is a must-see landmark in Marrakesh. Tickets cost 50 MAD (as of July 2026), and I highly recommend arriving early in the morning to beat the crowds. The central courtyard is the main highlight, featuring a beautiful pool, elegant porticos, mesmerizing stucco details, and intricate Arabic calligraphy. Up on the top floor, you can explore the old student dormitories, which offer fantastic photo opportunities.

Istituto antico, risalente al XVI secolo, ristrutturato in modo fantastico. È disposto su 2 piani dove si possono visitare le aule studio, una stanza adibita agli oggetti di vita oltre al Corano e un’altra stanza adibita a museo. Infine, una stanza è adibita al filmato relativo alla ristrutturazione avvenuta. Molto bello

The Madrasa Ben Youssef is a very pleasant, beautiful, and remarkably well-maintained historical site. I was absolutely fascinated by the architecture, especially the stunning dominance of intricately carved wood throughout the building.

​The only downside was the timing; by around 11:00 AM, the crowds became quite massive, which took away from the peaceful atmosphere and made it feel a bit too bustling. Despite the hustle and bustle, it remains a gorgeous and must-see historical landmark. I highly recommend visiting as early as possible to fully appreciate its beauty in quiet!

La Madrasa Ben Youssef è una antica scuola coranica, perfettamente restaurata. Il complesso è ricco di decorazioni, intarsi, stucchi e soprattutto mosaici colorati che rendono ogni angolo speciale. Il cortile interno è probabilmente la parte più suggestiva e caratteristica.

La Madrasa di Ben Youssef è uno dei monumenti storici più affascinanti di Marrakech, in Marocco. Fondata nel XIV secolo e ricostruita nel XVI secolo sotto la dinastia saadiana, è stata per secoli la scuola islamica (madrasa) più grande del Nord Africa, arrivando a ospitare fino a 900 studenti.
Oggi è celebre in tutto il mondo come capolavoro dell'architettura arabo-andalusa. I visitatori rimangono incantati dal suo splendido cortile centrale, decorato con raffinati mosaici colorati (zellij), spettacolari stucchi e dettagli in legno di cedro intagliato a mano. È un'oasi di pace e di straordinaria bellezza artistica nel cuore della medina.

Bin Yusuf Medresesi, 16. yüzyılda Saadi Hanedanı döneminde inşa edilen ve uzun yıllar Kuzey Afrika’nın en önemli İslam eğitim merkezlerinden biri olarak hizmet veren etkileyici bir yapı. Bir dönem yüzlerce öğrencinin eğitim gördüğü medrese, bugün Fas’ın zengin tarihini ve sanat anlayışını yansıtan en önemli eserlerden biri. Avlusundaki mermer havuz, ince zellige mozaikler, sedir ağacından yapılmış oymalar ve zarif alçı işlemeleri olağanüstü bir işçiliğe sahip. Yapının her köşesinde dönemin mimari estetiğini ve ustalığını hissetmek mümkün. Sessiz atmosferi sayesinde geçmişe kısa bir yolculuk yapıyormuş hissi veriyor. Marakeş’e gelenlerin hem tarihi yakından tanımak hem de Fas mimarisinin en güzel örneklerinden birini görmek için mutlaka ziyaret etmesi gereken yerlerden biri.

Agafay Desert is truly a must-do experience when visiting Marrakech, Morocco. Just a short drive from the city, you’re transported into a stunning rocky desert landscape that feels calm, magical, and completely different from the busy medina.

The experience started with an adrenaline-filled quad bike ride, racing across the vast desert terrain with breathtaking views all around. It was exciting, fun, and perfect for adventure lovers. After that came a peaceful camel ride at sunset, which felt very traditional and gave us time to soak in the beauty of the desert as the sky changed colors.

The evening ended with an unforgettable desert dinner and live show. The food was delicious, well presented, and filling, and the entertainment made the night even more special. From music and dancing to fire performances, the atmosphere was lively, warm, and beautifully organised under the stars.

Agafay Desert offers the perfect mix of adventure, culture, and relaxation. Whether you’re visiting Marrakech for the first time or returning, this experience is absolutely worth it and one you’ll remember long after your trip ends.

Agafay desert is a must whenever you visit Marrakech.
The quad biking is an absolute blast with the Atlas Mountains as your backdrop. It’s loud, messy, and a total adrenaline rush but not too fast. Then, you swap the engine for a camel ride to catch the sunset. I'll be honest: camels are bumpy and a little awkward when they stand up. They are tied in a line closer together so you might feel a camel’s mouth on your thigh. Don’t be alarmed. It’s definitely a popular tourist circuit, so don't expect to be the only person out there, but between the dust, the mint tea, and that sunset, it’s a really good experience you shouldn't skip. Just a heads-up: wear long pants and bring a scarf, or can you buy it at one of the stalls there. Buy the ticket for the full experience on GetYourGuide which is actually cheaper (£16 pp)
